name: ERF
description:
ERF (Encapsulated Resource File) is a single-file archive used by KotOR I & II. It bundles many game resources—scripts, models, textures, sounds, and so on—into one file. The same format is used for MOD (mod) and SAV (save) files; the 4-byte file type at the start tells you which.

How it's laid out: a fixed 160-byte header at the beginning, then up to four blocks in order—localized description strings (optional), the key list, the resource list, and the raw resource data. The header stores the number of resources (entryCount) and the file offset of each block, so you can jump straight to the key list or resource list without scanning.

Parsing: Read the header first. Use offsetToKeyList and entryCount to read the key list (entryCount × 24 bytes). Use offsetToResourceList and entryCount to read the resource list (entryCount × 8 bytes). Key at index i and resource at index i refer to the same resource: the key gives you the name (resRef) and type (resType), the resource entry gives you the file offset and size in bytes. To extract resource i, read size bytes starting at that offset from the beginning of the file.

Writing: Lay out the file as header (160 bytes), then (if used) the localized string block, then the key list, then the resource list, then the concatenated resource data. Compute each block's offset from the previous one and its size; write the header last with the correct offsets and counts, or reserve space and patch them in. All multi-byte integers are little-endian.

Header.description: 160 bytes at the start of the file. The first two fields are 4-byte magic strings: file type ("ERF ", "MOD ", or "SAV ") and version (e.g. "V1.0"). The next seven u32s are counts and offsets you need for parsing: languageCount and localizedStringSize describe the optional localized description block; entryCount is how many resources are in the archive; the three offsets tell you where the localized strings, key list, and resource list start. The rest are metadata (build date, description TLK ref) and reserved space.

fields.name: fileType
fields.description: Archive type: 'ERF ', 'MOD ', or 'SAV ' (4 chars, space-padded).

fields.name: fileVersion
fields.description: Format version; e.g. 'V1.0'.

fields.name: languageCount
fields.description: Number of localized description strings (one per language). 0 if no description block.

fields.name: localizedStringSize
fields.description: Total size in bytes of the localized string block. 0 if not used.

fields.name: entryCount
fields.description: Number of resources in the archive; length of both the key list and the resource list.

fields.name: offsetToLocalizedString
fields.description: File offset where the localized string block starts (usually 160). Ignore if localizedStringSize is 0.

fields.name: offsetToKeyList
fields.description: File offset of the key list. Key list size = entryCount × 24 bytes.

fields.name: offsetToResourceList
fields.description: File offset of the resource list. Resource list size = entryCount × 8 bytes. Resource data starts immediately after this block.

fields.name: buildYear
fields.description: Build year (e.g. years since 1900); metadata only.

fields.name: buildDay
fields.description: Day of year (1–366); metadata only.

fields.name: DescriptionStrRef
fields.description: TLK string ref for archive description when localized strings are not used.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved; keep zero when writing.

LocalizedString.description: One localized string in the description block: a language ID (u32), the string length in bytes (u32), then that many bytes of text. The block contains languageCount of these in a row; total byte size is localizedStringSize. Used for the archive's description in multiple languages (e.g. for the mod manager).

fields.name: languageId
fields.description: Which language this string is for; see LanguageId.

fields.name: stringSize
fields.description: Length of the following string in bytes.

fields.name: value
fields.description: String bytes (no null terminator).

LanguageId.description: Language IDs used in the localized string block and elsewhere in KotOR. Matches dialog.tlk and other localized content.

KeyEntry.description: One key entry (24 bytes). Identifies a resource by name and type. The key list has entryCount entries; key index i corresponds to resource list index i. resRef is the resource name without extension (e.g. "my_script"), null-padded to 16 bytes—trim nulls when displaying or comparing. resId must equal the key's index (it points into the resource list). resType is the game's resource type code (e.g. script, model, texture); use a resource type table to map to extensions.

fields.name: resRef
fields.description: Resource name, no extension, null-padded to 16 chars. Trim trailing nulls.

fields.name: resId
fields.description: Index into the resource list (must match this key's index in the key list).

fields.name: resType
fields.description: Resource type ID (script, model, etc.).

fields.name: unused
fields.description: Reserved; keep 0 when writing.

ResourceEntry.description: One resource entry (8 bytes). Gives the location and size of the resource's raw bytes in the file. The resource list has entryCount entries; index i goes with key list index i. offset is the byte offset from the start of the file; size is how many bytes to read. Resource data starts at offset and runs for size bytes. When writing, pack all resource payloads back-to-back after the resource list and fill this table with each resource's offset and size.

fields.name: offset
fields.description: File offset (from start of file) where this resource's data begins.

fields.name: size
fields.description: Length of this resource's data in bytes.

sections.name: header
sections.description: Fixed 160-byte header. Read this first to get entryCount and the three offsets. Check fileType to confirm you have an ERF/MOD/SAV and fileVersion for format compatibility.

sections.name: localizedStrings
sections.description: Optional block of localized description strings. If localizedStringSize is 0, skip it. Otherwise start at offsetToLocalizedString and read languageCount entries in sequence: each entry is languageId (u32), stringSize (u32), then stringSize bytes. Total bytes read should equal localizedStringSize.

sections.name: keyList
sections.description: Table of resource keys: entryCount entries, 24 bytes each. Entry i gives you the name (resRef) and type (resType) for resource i. To list all resources, iterate i from 0 to entryCount−1 and read KeyEntry at offsetToKeyList + i×24.

sections.name: resourceList
sections.description: Table of resource locations: entryCount entries, 8 bytes each. Entry i gives offset and size for resource i. The actual resource bytes start at offset (from the start of the file) and are size bytes long. Resource data for the whole archive begins at file offset (offsetToResourceList + entryCount×8); each resource's offset in this table points somewhere in that region (or later). To extract resource i: read ResourceEntry at offsetToResourceList + i×8, then read size bytes from the file at offset.
